prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm in a training where I'll build a working memory around one live challenge I'

risks_fired.evidence: "Ask me these in turn" is too soft per check_prompts.md rule 15, but body prose immediately after the fence names the failure mode and gives a recovery move: "If it shows all three questions at once, push back and ask for one at a time." Body-callout carve-out applies — downgraded from high to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Body callout is present and qualifies under the carve-out; optionally strengthen by adding structural enforcement inside the fence: "Ask one question at a time. Wait for my answer before asking the next. Do not show me the full list."

risks_fired.evidence: Conversational opening ("Help me pin the challenge down") invites a brief narration before Q1 lands.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Low risk; acceptable as-is. Optional: add a body note "Claude will usually acknowledge before asking — the first question comes right after."

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Based on the challenge brief you just wrote, suggest where I'd go looking for ra

risks_fired.evidence: Prompt asks for named practitioners with no link request (prior version had "with one link each if you know them" — that text removed; risk dropped from high to low). Residual: practitioner names may be generic or poorly matched to the student's specific challenge rather than actually relevant practitioners.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional body callout: "Claude's practitioner suggestions may be generic — treat as a starting point; search on your own for who's actually working on this problem now." Or add an anchor inside the fence: "Avoid generic domain names that would fit any challenge in this space."

risks_fired.evidence: "Keep it short" may be satisfied by broadly-recognisable, safe suggestions rather than genuinely challenge-specific names.
